indexes.name: idx_lineitem_shipdate_l_orderkey
indexes.workload_pattern: Q3, Q5, Q6, Q7, Q9, Q11, Q13, Q14, Q18, Q19 include predicates on lineitem.l_shipdate and lineitem joins to orders via lineitem.l_orderkey = orders.o_orderkey.
indexes.benefits: Supports efficient range filtering on l_shipdate while also accelerating subsequent lookups/probing by l_orderkey for the join to orders.
indexes.tradeoffs: Extra write overhead and larger index than a single-column l_shipdate index.

indexes.name: idx_orders_custkey_orderdate
indexes.workload_pattern: Q3 joins customer->orders on customer.c_custkey = orders.o_custkey and filters orders by orders.o_orderdate. Q9 filters orders by o_orderdate range and joins via o_custkey.
indexes.benefits: Speeds up finding orders for a given customer within specific order-date ranges.
indexes.tradeoffs: Additional write overhead; may be less beneficial if queries filter only by orderdate (already covered by existing orders(o_orderdate)).

indexes.name: idx_lineitem_shipdate_discount_quantity
indexes.workload_pattern: Q5 filters lineitem by l_shipdate range and also applies l_discount BETWEEN -0.95 AND 1.02 and l_quantity < 21.
indexes.benefits: Improves selectivity for Q5 by combining the date range with the discount and quantity predicates, reducing rows scanned/aggregated.
indexes.tradeoffs: Larger index and more maintenance overhead; may provide diminishing returns if the selectivity of predicates is already high.

indexes.name: idx_lineitem_shipdate_partial_q1
indexes.workload_pattern: Q1 repeatedly applies l_shipdate <= date('1995-02-13','-90 days') for the main aggregation filter.
indexes.benefits: Smaller, more selective index for the dominant upper-bound predicate in Q1.
indexes.tradeoffs: Partial index is only useful when the query predicate matches the same logical condition; additional write overhead.

global_observations: Kept only PostgreSQL-valid index types and removed suggestions that were either inconsistent with the provided metadata/workload or redundant with existing indexes.
Focused suggestions on lineitem date-range filtering combined with join/filter columns, plus one partial index for Q1's explicit l_shipdate upper-bound.
ontology_notes: Adjusted index suggestions to be PostgreSQL-consistent: removed the orders(o_orderstatus,o_orderdate) suggestion (insufficient direct predicate evidence from the given workload and potentially redundant with existing o_orderdate indexing). Replaced the invalid/overly speculative partial index predicate expression with a valid PostgreSQL WHERE expression for the Q1 l_shipdate upper-bound. Expanded the l_shipdate+discount+quantity composite to better match Q5’s combined filtering predicates and maintained correct rules_triggered usage.
